category.text: $course$/Advanced Algebra (M.Sc. Mathematics)/Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: Applied (Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions): solve \( {a}x={b} \).

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

generalfeedback.text: Both lines have same LHS but different RHS \({c}\ne{d}\): the system is inconsistent (no solution).

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: For 2x2 system \({a}x+y={c},\ x+{b}y={d}\), Cramer gives \(\Delta\) (coefficient determinant) equal to:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: For lines \({a}x+{b}y={c}\) and \({a}x+{b}y={d}\) with \({c}\ne{d}\), the system has:

generalfeedback.text: Same coefficients, different RHS \(\Rightarrow\) parallel non-coincident lines \(\Rightarrow\) no solution.

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: LCM of \({a}\) and \({b}\) when HCF\((a,b)=h\) equals:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

generalfeedback.text: Always commutative and associative; forms abelian group.

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: Area of parallelogram with base \({a}\) and height \({b}\):

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: In a group, the inverse of each element is:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: If cost price is \({a}\) and selling price is \({b}\) (\({b}>{a}\)), profit % is:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: Median of an even-count sorted list:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: A field is a commutative ring with:

generalfeedback.text: Every non-zero element has multiplicative inverse.

answer.text: multiplicative inverse for all non-zero elements

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions
Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: In a recurring deposit of \({a}\) per month for \(T\) months, total deposit is:

name.text: Chapter 4 — Unit IV — Field extensions, algebraic and transcendental extensions

questiontext.text: A homogeneous system \(AX=0\) has a non-trivial solution if and only if:

generalfeedback.text: Non-trivial solution exists iff \(A\) is singular: \(\det A=0\).
